How do labor market reforms reshape household consumption?
items.display_why: The clearest nearby routes run through employment, unemployment, and productivity shocks.
items.display_first_step: Start with reform episodes that changed job turnover sharply, then trace whether household spending moved with them.

items.display_title: When does product innovation translate into real wage gains?
items.display_why: Nearby papers connect innovation to pay through employment, productivity, and labor productivity.
items.display_first_step: Start with sectors where product upgrading can be matched to wage records and compare gains across worker groups.

items.display_title: How might AI widen or narrow the gender wage gap?
items.display_why: The closest local routes run through wage distribution, productivity, and uneven AI adoption across jobs.
items.display_first_step: Compare occupations with uneven AI exposure and track whether pay gaps move where adoption arrives first.

items.display_title: When does national health insurance change earnings?
items.display_why: Nearby papers connect insurance to earnings through place of residence, hours worked, and education.
items.display_first_step: Start with coverage expansions that changed enrollment quickly and follow labor supply and earnings after take-up.

items.display_title: When do fiscal rules start to bite in employment?
items.display_why: The strongest nearby routes run through fiscal policy, fiscal consolidation, and government spending.
items.display_first_step: Use rule changes or enforcement shocks to compare labor-market adjustment before and after tighter budget constraints.

items.display_title: How do tariffs change demand for labor?
items.display_why: Nearby papers already connect tariffs to labor demand through domestic prices and adjustment costs.
items.display_first_step: Start with tariff shifts that moved input prices and test which firms or workers adjusted first.

items.display_title: When do firing costs widen income inequality?
items.display_why: The local routes run through wage inequality, unemployment, and employment churn.
items.display_first_step: Compare reforms that changed dismissal costs and trace whether losses concentrated at the bottom of the distribution.

items.display_title: How does fiscal policy show up in health-related quality of life?
items.display_why: Nearby papers connect fiscal policy to quality of life through unemployment, economic activity, and income.
items.display_first_step: Start with large fiscal contractions or expansions and test whether health-related quality-of-life measures move after them.

items.display_title: How does inflation reshape wage dispersion?
items.display_why: The clearest nearby routes run through unemployment, labor productivity, and tax incidence.
items.display_first_step: Look for inflation episodes with strong price pass-through and test whether wage gaps widen unevenly across firms or sectors.

items.display_title: When do R&D efforts translate into labor productivity?
items.display_why: Nearby papers link R&D to labor productivity through product innovation, technical change, and firm-level efficiency.
items.display_first_step: Start with settings where R&D spending and worker-level output can be tracked together over time.
